name: Salt de la Portellada Trail
name_original: Salto de la Portellada sobre el Río Tastavins
description: This easy 4.0-mile (6.5 km) hike leads to the stunning 20-meter Portellada Waterfall in Matarraña, perfect for families and nature lovers.
region_intro: Hiking around La Portellada offers trails through a landscape characterized by river valleys, forested areas, and natural water features. The region features varied terrain, including paths along streams and routes leading to waterfalls. Hikers can explore a mix of open countryside and shaded woodland, with elevation changes typical of the area. Best hiking trails around La Portellada The most popular hiking route is Salt de la Portellada Trail, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This easy path leads to the prominent Salt de la Portellada waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Portellada Waterfall loop from La Portellada, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.7 km) path. This circular route explores the area around the waterfall and surrounding natural features. Local hikers also love the Monastery in the Forest – View of La Torre del Comte loop from La Fresneda, a 9.6 miles (15.5 km) trail leading through forested landscapes and offering views of La Torre del Comte, often completed in about 4 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around La Portellada is defined by river valleys, dense woodlands, and impressive waterfalls. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in La Portellada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore La Portellada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Portellada?
A: La Portellada offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. This includes 8 easy routes, 26 moderate paths, and 8 more challenging trails.

Q: Are there easy hikes suitable for beginners in La Portellada?
A: Yes, La Portellada has several easy trails perfect for beginners. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974451608/salt-de-la-portellada-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de la Portellada Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.0-mile (6.5 km) path that leads directly to the prominent Salt de la Portellada waterfall.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in La Portellada?
A: Yes, many trails in La Portellada are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall loop from La Portellada&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.7-mile (10.7 km) route that explores the area around the waterfall and its natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in La Portellada?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974451608/salt-de-la-portellada-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de la Portellada Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabosa Pool&lt;/a&gt;, another beautiful waterfall, in the area.

Q: What are some family-friendly hiking options in La Portellada?
A: For families, the easier trails are generally recommended. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974451608/salt-de-la-portellada-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de la Portellada Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice due to its manageable length and the engaging destination of the waterfall. Always check the specific trail's difficulty and length to ensure it suits your family's abilities.

Q: Is La Portellada a good place for dog-friendly walks?
A: Many trails in La Portellada are suitable for walking with dogs, offering varied terrain through river valleys and woodlands. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near wildlife or livestock, and to check local regulations for specific areas or natural parks you plan to visit.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while hiking in La Portellada?
A: Beyond the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, hikers can discover other natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabosa Pool&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3499189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool of El Azud&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Pena&lt;/a&gt;, a local reservoir, also offers scenic views and is accessible via some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Portellada?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in La Portellada, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler, wetter periods.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in La Portellada?
A: Parking is typically available near the starting points of popular trails and in the villages surrounding La Portellada. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find designated parking areas within or just outside La Portellada village itself.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Portellada?
A: The hiking trails in La Portellada are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's beautiful river valleys, the impressive waterfalls, and the well-maintained paths that make exploring enjoyable.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, La Portellada offers difficult routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981374145/la-portellada-fornoles-santuario-de-monserrate-pr-te-161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portellada - Fórnoles - Santuario de Monserrate (PR-TE 161)&lt;/a&gt;. This trail spans nearly 12.3 miles (19.8 km) with significant elevation changes, providing a more strenuous experience.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of historical sites or landmarks?
A: While primarily focused on natural beauty, some routes offer glimpses of local landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14889128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery in the Forest – View of La Torre del Comte loop from La Fresneda&lt;/a&gt; provides views of La Torre del Comte. Additionally, nearby towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beceite&lt;/a&gt; and its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt; are accessible for exploration.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region's natural water features provide opportunities for wild swimming. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabosa Pool&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3499189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool of El Azud&lt;/a&gt; are popular spots where hikers can cool off during warmer months. Always exercise caution and check local conditions before swimming.
tips: In 2023 the waterfall was dry, but the eroded rocks provide a beautiful picture.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Salt de la Portellada Trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically reach the vicinity of the trail by car. From the main TE-V-3004 road, look for a signposted detour that guides you down a forest path. Parking is usually available near the trailhead where this path begins.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The Salt de la Portellada Trail is considered easy and has no significant difficulty, making it suitable for hikers of all experience levels, including beginners and families. The elevation change is minor.

faqs.question: How long does the hike typically take?
faqs.answer: The main route to the waterfall is approximately 4 km (2.5 miles) round trip from the road junction and usually takes about 1 hour to complete. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the waterfall.

faqs.question: Is the Salt de la Portellada Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. It's a natural area with forest roads and tracks, which are usually suitable for dogs. Please ensure your dog is kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and for the comfort of other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is often after rainfall, especially in spring or early summer, when the Tastavins River's flow is abundant, making the Portellada Waterfall particularly impressive. Warmer months are also popular for enjoying the natural pool at the base of the waterfall.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Salt de la Portellada Trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits required or entrance fees to access the Salt de la Portellada Trail or the waterfall. It's a publicly accessible natural area.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows forest roads and tracks. Some sections may involve crossing the Tastavins River, which could mean getting your feet wet, especially after heavy rains. Paths leading down to the pool beneath the waterfall might involve navigating around detached rock blocks. You'll walk through areas with scattered pines, willows, poplars, and cultivated olive and almond groves.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main highlight is the impressive 20-meter high Portellada Waterfall, formed by the Tastavins River. You'll also see unique rock formations sculpted by river erosion, lush vegetation including pine and oak forests, Mediterranean scrubland, and agricultural terraces. Natural viewpoints offer panoramic views of the plateau and the waterfall. The turquoise pool at the base of the waterfall is also a notable feature.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I observe on the trail?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species such as common crested larks, corn buntings, raptors like kestrels, black crows, magpies, ravens, wagtails, and sandpipers. Keep an eye out for signs of otters along the riverbanks, as they are known to inhabit the area.

faqs.question: Is the Salt de la Portellada Trail part of a larger park or protected area?
faqs.answer: The Salt de la Portellada Trail is located within the beautiful Matarraña region of Teruel, often referred to as 'Spanish Tuscany' due to its picturesque landscapes. While not a formally designated national park with strict entrance regulations, the area is valued for its natural beauty and is part of the broader Comarca del Matarraña.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for swimming?
faqs.answer: Yes, the natural pool at the base of the Portellada Waterfall is a popular spot for swimming and bathing, especially during warmer months. The waters are known for their beautiful turquoise color.
